15.reason: [The space after %d in scanf can cause input parsing issues, especially when next input is immediately following without whitespace. It may consume extra whitespace unnecessarily and interfere with subsequent reads.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [The space after %d in scanf can cause input parsing issues, especially when next input is immediately following without whitespace. It may consume extra whitespace unnecessarily and interfere with subsequent reads.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [The space after %d in scanf can cause input parsing issues by consuming extra whitespace unexpectedly, potentially skipping next operation tokens]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [The format string "%d " consumes trailing whitespace, which may cause the next input to be read prematurely or block waiting for extra input. It should be "%d" without the space.]

21.reason: [Incrementing i beyond 100 leads to invalid stack state; once stack is full, further pushes should not alter the stack pointer. This causes i to become 101, breaking future logic.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Incrementing i beyond 100 leads to invalid stack state; once stack is full, further pushes should not alter i. Also, this causes i to become 101, which breaks future logic.]

31.reason: [This check comes too late; i was already decremented, so if i was 0, it becomes -1, triggering "error", but the pop operation should have been rejected before decrementing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This check comes too late; i was already decremented, so if i was 0, it becomes -1, triggering "error", but the pop operation should have been rejected before decrementing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [i cannot be <0 here because i!=0 ensures i>=1 before decrement, so i>=0 after; this check is redundant]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [i is decremented before this check, so if i was 0, it becomes -1, but the outer if(i!=0) prevents that path; however, if i was 1, it becomes 0 and passes, so this i<0 check is unreachable and incorrect logic for underflow]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [This check comes too late鈥攊 was already decremented, so if i was 0, it becomes -1 and triggers "error", but the pop operation should have been rejected before decrementing.]
